subject: Human Resources
title: Global HR Challenges
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Global HR Challenges.

hook.text: A company operates in 30 countries. Each has different labor laws, holidays, benefits, and cultural norms.

buildup.text: Global HR manages people across borders—navigating legal systems, languages, currencies, and time zones.

discovery.text: What's standard in one country may be illegal in another. Mandatory benefits in France differ wildly from the U.S.

twist.text: Cultural differences matter as much as legal ones. Direct feedback in the U.S. can be offensive in Japan.

climax.text: Successful global HR balances consistency in values with flexibility in local practices.

punchline.text: One company, many countries, countless HR puzzles.

quiz.question: What makes global HR particularly challenging?
options: Different laws, cultures, and practices across countries
Every country has the same labor laws
